skill.name: writing-plans
skill.description: Create detailed engineering implementation plans with granular, testable tasks before feature work, refactoring, or multi-file changes.
skill.summary: Turn complex engineering work into clear implementation plans with small TDD-oriented tasks.
skill.author: DMJGilbert

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks and weak-crypto patterns, but review found no executable scripts, shell invocation, cryptographic code, network access, or prompt injection. The skill is a documentation-only planning guide and is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown inline code and fenced examples that show file paths, task templates, and commit message text. They do not instruct the assistant to run shell commands or execute user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The marked locations are inside Markdown documentation or examples. No shell API, executable script, or instruction to execute commands is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings do not correspond to any hashing, encryption, signing, or password-handling code. The referenced lines are prose, headings, or example plan text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic operation at the flagged lines. The matches are semantic false positives in ordinary Markdown content.

content.value_statement: Complex engineering work often fails when tasks are too broad or hard to verify. This skill creates structured implementation plans with small TDD steps, affected files, and clear checks.
actual_capabilities: Creates implementation plans for features, refactors, and multi-file changes.
Structures plans with goals, architecture notes, tech stack, and affected files.
Breaks work into small tasks intended to take two to five minutes each.
Promotes test-first development with failing tests before implementation.
Adds verification checklists for each task.
Suggests execution options after the plan is written.
limitations: It does not inspect code unless the host assistant reads project files separately.
It does not execute the plan or run tests by itself.
It assumes the project can support test-first development.
It provides planning guidance, not architecture approval.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Feature
use_cases.description: Break a product request into small implementation tasks with tests and verification steps.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Refactor
use_cases.description: Map affected files and create safe, committable tasks before changing shared code.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Agent Work
use_cases.description: Create task boundaries that can be assigned to separate sessions or subagents.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Feature Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for adding [feature]. Include goal, architecture, affected files, tasks, and verification checks.

prompt_templates.title: TDD Task Breakdown
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a TDD implementation plan for [change]. Make each task small, testable, independently verifiable, and ready for a focused commit.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a refactoring plan for [area]. Identify affected files, preserve behavior, add tests first, and include rollback-friendly task boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Agent Execution Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a granular implementation plan for [project]. Organize tasks so separate agents can execute them with minimal shared context.

output_examples.input: Plan an avatar component with fallback initials.
output_examples.output: A plan with a concise goal, React and TypeScript architecture notes, affected component and test files, and two small TDD tasks.

output_examples.input: Plan a safe refactor of authentication validation.
output: A file impact list for validators, tests, and integration points.
Task steps that add characterization tests before behavior changes.
Verification checks for passing tests and unchanged error handling.

output_examples.input: Plan work that multiple agents can execute.
output_examples.output: A sequence of isolated tasks with file ownership, expected verification, and clear handoff points between sessions.

best_practices: Read the relevant code before writing the plan when file impact is uncertain.
Keep each task small enough to verify independently.
Include tests and acceptance checks before implementation steps.
anti_patterns: Do not create broad tasks like implement the whole feature.
Do not skip affected files when the change spans multiple modules.
Do not plan speculative features that are outside the stated goal.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It writes implementation plans that another assistant or developer can execute.

faq.question: Where should plans be saved?
faq.answer: The skill recommends saving plans under docs/plans with a date and feature name.

faq.question: Does it require TDD?
faq.answer: Yes. The guidance emphasizes writing failing tests before implementation where practical.

faq.question: Can it support refactoring work?
faq.answer: Yes. It is intended for features, complex refactors, and changes across multiple files.

faq.question: Can teams use it with multiple agents?
faq.answer: Yes. It encourages granular tasks that can be assigned to separate sessions or subagents.

faq.question: Does it run commands or access the network?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file contains only Markdown planning instructions.
